---
title: "Loan Portfolio Risk Dashboard"
author: Elvis Safary
format:
    dashboard:
        embed-resources: true
        orientation: rows
theme: sandstone
execute:
    echo: false
    warning: false
---

<style>
.value-box-value { 
    font-size: 1.3rem !important; /* Smaller font to fit 5 boxes */
    font-weight: 700 !important; 
}
.value-box-title { 
    font-size: 0.7rem !important; /* Smaller title */
    font-weight: 600 !important; 
    text-transform: uppercase; 
}
</style>


```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import plotly.express as px
from itables import show
import itables.options as opt

#load Dataset
loan_data = pd.read_csv('loans_full_schema.csv')

#clean and prepare
#create derived metrics
loan_data['loan_to_income_ratio'] = loan_data['loan_amount'] / loan_data['annual_income']
loan_data['interest_burden_index'] = loan_data['interest_rate'] * loan_data['debt_to_income']
loan_data['repayment_ratio'] = loan_data['paid_principal'] / loan_data['loan_amount']

#convert issue_month to datetime
loan_data['issue_date'] = pd.to_datetime(loan_data['issue_month'], format='%b-%Y')

#KPI calculations
avg_loan_amount = loan_data['loan_amount'].mean()
avg_interest_rate = loan_data['interest_rate'].mean()
total_fully_paid = (loan_data['loan_status'] == 'Fully Paid').sum()
default_late_list = ['Late (31-120 days)', 'Late (16-30 days)', 'Charged Off']
pct_default_late = (loan_data['loan_status'].isin(default_late_list).sum() / len(loan_data)) * 100
avg_dti = loan_data['debt_to_income'].mean()
total_loans = len(loan_data)

#visualizations
#bar chart: Avg interest rate by grade
avg_int_grade = loan_data.groupby('grade')['interest_rate'].mean().reset_index()

fig_bar = px.bar(avg_int_grade, x='grade', y='interest_rate', color='grade', text_auto= '.1f',  color_discrete_sequence=px.colors.qualitative.Safe, title="Average Interest Rate by Credit Grade")

fig_bar.update_layout(showlegend=False,    yaxis_title="Avg Interest Rate (%)",    xaxis_title="Loan Grade")

fig_bar.update_traces(textposition='outside', 
cliponaxis=False)


#pie Chart: distribution of loan purposes
fig_pie = px.pie(loan_data, names='loan_purpose', title="Distribution of Loan Purposes", hole=0.4)

#| title: "Risk/Reward: Loan Amount vs. Interest Rate"
fig_scatter = px.scatter(loan_data, x='loan_amount',     y='interest_rate', color='grade',   marginal_x='box', marginal_y='violin',  
title="Risk/Reward: Loan Amount vs. Interest Rate", 
opacity=0.5, labels={'loan_amount': 'Loan Amount ($)', 'interest_rate': 'Interest Rate (%)'})

# Stacked Column Chart: Loan status by application type
fig_stacked = px.histogram(loan_data, x='application_type', color='loan_status', barmode='stack', title="Loan Performance by Application Type")

#line chart: Monthly issuance trends by grade
monthly_trends = loan_data.groupby(['issue_date', 'grade']).size().reset_index(name='loan_count')

fig_line = px.line(monthly_trends, x='issue_date', y='loan_count', color='grade',                  title="Monthly Issuance Volume by Grade", markers=True)
```

# Overview
## Row {height="20%"}

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Average Loan Amount"
dict(icon="cash-stack", color="primary", value=f"${avg_loan_amount:,.0f}")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Average Interest Rate"
dict(icon="globe", color="info", value=f"{avg_interest_rate:.1f}%")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Fully Paid Loans"
dict(icon="check-circle", color="success", value=f"{total_fully_paid:,}")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Default/Late Rate"
dict(icon="exclamation-triangle", color="danger", value=f"{pct_default_late:.2f}%")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Average Debt-to-Income"
dict(icon="wallet2", color="warning", value=f"{avg_dti:.1f}%")
```


## Row {height="80%"}
### Column {width="50%"}

```{python}
fig_bar
```

### Column {width="50%"}

```{python}
fig_pie
```

# Risk Analysis
## Row {height="15%"}

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Total Borrowers"
dict(icon="person-plus", color="info", value=f"{total_loans:,}")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Average Interest Burden Index"
dict(icon="speedometer2", color="secondary", value=f"{loan_data['interest_burden_index'].mean():.0f}")
```

## Row {height="85%"}

### Column {width="50%"}
```{python}
fig_scatter
```

### Column {width="50%"}

```{python}
fig_stacked
```

# Data Source and Summary
*Summary of Findings*

Credit Risk & Rates: There is a linear correlation between loan grade and interest rate, with Grade G borrowers paying nearly triple the interest of Grade A borrowers.

Default Concentration: While the overall default/late rate is low (1.11%), these cases are concentrated in lower credit grades and specific purposes like "small business."

Debt Consolidation Dominance: The majority of the portfolio consists of debt consolidation loans, indicating a high demand for credit refinancing among borrowers.

Application Insights: Individual applications significantly outweigh joint applications, but joint applications tend to show slightly different status distributions, suggesting a diversification of risk.


```{python}
opt.classes = ["display", "nowrap", "compact"]
show(loan_data[['state', 'annual_income', 'loan_amount', 'grade', 'interest_rate', 'loan_status', 'loan_purpose']], paging=True, buttons=['csv', 'excel'])
```
